item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, that means a lot! I'm very happy about the response this has gotten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting! I liked that fact that you tied other form of SCP’s in the thread, in a way expanding the universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, thank you! I'm glad you enjoyed this article, it was a blast organizing the information and deciding what goes where.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the conciseness of the page. Many authors would really drag out the description and cram it with useless filler but, in contrast, the meat of this article was concise and easily digestible, and the addends were quite smoothly concatenated with the main text. I also felt a subtle nod toward 035 with the porcelain masks.

Really enjoyed reading this; thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should’ve mentioned this in my previous comment but I really like this. Thing Syncope to the Hanged King and Algadda, and even throwing in more instances of 035? Great stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heyya! So, we explain it a little bit up thread, but the issue is that we can't set Neutralized to a Secondary Class. In fact, I think I'm gonna edit that. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Had this on my to-read list for a bit and finally got around to it. I enjoyed it a lot, but why is it classed as Decommissioned when the Foundation didn't intentionally destroy it and it's referenced as Neutralized at the end?

Regardless, I've included it on the esoteric classes list as Decommissioned. If you change up the classification feel free to adjust the guide accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm absolutely thrilled at this interpretation, these were exactly my thoughts while designing them. I'm glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this: rockstar refugees from Hellscape Alagadda… who are actually decent folks, just wanting to play their own music as free as they can from the influence of the Ambassador. On the other hand, though, they are still Hanged King devotees, and as the ending shows, that still makes them a danger…
It's the dissonance between sympathy for people who just want to do their own things and horror at their devotion to the Hanged King (something which we all know does not end well) that makes this SCP interesting.

That, plus how these Alagaddan performers have unintentionally become the Godfathers of Punk Rock. One SCP: one new culture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, glad you liked it, Sketchy. I'm a little iffy on the crosslink too, I'll see what snuggly thinks.

Edit: Remov'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FWIW: the Tech team usually handles tagging an article and it's generally advised for authors to not manually tag stuff with e.g, those tags so that none are missed. Lacking tags at this stage is fine :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A few tags seem relevant here. Cognitohazard, class-of-76, hanged-king, humanoid, music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Johnny Rotten? Interesting…

Pretty good article. I like how the band connects to several other anomalies, although I feel iffy about the 035 crosslink.

+1

item.title: Re: Author Post - (2)
item.content:encoded: This was an absolute pleasure to write, thank you so much for doing this with me, I enjoyed every night i stayed up till 5 to be on at the same time as you. To be honest, i was insanely clueless about punk when we came up with this idea on chat, and I listened to classic punk for three weeks. This collab was amazing, thanks.

item.title: Author Post - (1)
item.content:encoded:
Hello! This collaborative effort was brought to you by myself and snuggly face. It took a long time, but we finally go there.

There were a lot of stressors and roadblocks along the way- snuggly's going off to the military, I have school, yada yada- so I'm absolutely thrilled that we managed to get this done and posted as soon as we did.

Thanks to our critters, especially torcsandantlers and cybersqyd, who were both massive helps.

I hope you enjoy reading, and I hope this article is successful. This would be snuggly's second article, and my first.
